title: Shadow at Netherwood
openingIncident: At 8:55 pm, Cassandra Finch, local historian, was found dead in the study of a rural manor house. Three people had been present during the final minutes, and each gave a different account. Nothing outside the room is needed to identify the killer.

suspects.name: Astrid Markham
suspects.statement: At 8:45 pm, Lila Rose helped me move the chair clear of the doorway. We left the room together immediately afterwards. Elijah Blackwood and Cassandra Finch were still inside.
suspects.alibiClaim: Lila Rose and I were together outside the room from 8:45 pm until the discovery at 8:55 pm.

suspects.name: Elijah Blackwood
suspects.statement: I left at 8:40 pm. I never touched the lamp and did not go near the book.
suspects.alibiClaim: I had already left the room by 8:40 pm and did not return.

suspects.name: Lila Rose
suspects.statement: At 8:45 pm, I helped Astrid Markham move the chair clear of the doorway. We then left together and remained together until the body was found at 8:55 pm.
suspects.alibiClaim: Astrid Markham and I were together outside the room from 8:45 pm until the discovery at 8:55 pm.

clues.title: The Dented Lamp
clues.detail: A fresh blood smear and a new impact dent mark the lamp. Cassandra Finch has a fresh blunt wound to the head.

clues.title: Movement of Chair
clues.detail: A fresh floor scrape made by the chair is crossed by a narrow blood droplet from the attack whose edge remains unbroken. Astrid Markham and Lila Rose both state that they moved the chair clear of the doorway at 8:45 pm before leaving together.

clues.title: Fibres on Lamp
clues.detail: Fine copper-coloured silk threads are caught inside a fresh split beneath the base of the lamp. Elijah Blackwood's copper silk pocket square has a fresh narrow tear, exposing the same alternating copper and navy stripe.

clues.title: Fragment at Book
clues.detail: A curved black lacquer chip edged in gold is lodged between the cover and first page of the book on top of a narrow blood droplet from the attack. The black-and-gold fountain-pen cap worn by Elijah Blackwood has a fresh curved gap in the lacquer; the exposed edges visibly fit the fragment.

clues.title: Crack in Mug
clues.detail: A crack runs across the mug. A continuous yellowed glue line is visible deep inside the break.

solution.motive: Elijah Blackwood killed Cassandra Finch because a long-buried family scandal was about to be exposed in the victim's forthcoming book.
solution.method: Cassandra Finch was killed by a heavy blow from the lamp.
solution.timeOfDeath: After 8:45 pm and before discovery at 8:55 pm
solution.opportunity: Elijah Blackwood claimed to have left at 8:40 pm. The timeline evidence places the attack after Astrid Markham and Lila Rose left together at 8:45 pm; the fine copper-coloured silk threads on the lamp and the black and gold fragment recovered from the book place Elijah Blackwood inside afterwards. Elijah Blackwood therefore had the only evidenced opportunity to be alone with Cassandra Finch and reach the lamp during the murder window.
